title: Lightweight ceramic thermal insulation materials
abstract: A thermal insulation panel is constructed from a hot face layer, an intermediate layer and an ambient face layer. Features providing insulating capability include the incorporation of an IR opacifier into the hot face layer, the provision of a low conduction volume such as an air gap between the hot face layer and the intermediate layer, and between the intermediate layer and the ambient face layer, and the use of infrared reflective foil on the back surface of the hot face layer and/or on the front surface of the intermediate layer. Base materials that may be used in the hot face layer include silica, alumina, and alumina-silica based ceramic materials. IR opacifiers in the hot face layer may contain ZrO 2 , SiC, rutile, TiO 2 , MnO, iron oxides, CrO 2 , ZrSiO 2 , Al 2 O 3 and mixtures thereof.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1 . (Original) A thermal insulation panel comprising: (a) a hot face layer having a front surface and a back surface, wherein the hot face layer comprises a material selected from the group consisting of silica, alumina, and an alumina-silica based ceramic material; (b) an intermediate layer having a front surface and a back surface, wherein the front surface of the intermediate layer is adjacent to the back surface of the hot face layer; and (c) an ambient face layer having a front surface and a back surface, wherein the front surface of the ambient face layer is adjacent to the back surface of the intermediate layer. 2. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , further comprising a glass fiber cloth layer having a front surface and a back surface, wherein the glass fiber layer cloth is interposed between the intermediate layer and the ambient face layer, the front surface of the glass fiber cloth layer is adjacent to the back surface of the intermediate layer; and the back surface of the glass fiber cloth layer is adjacent to the front surface of the ambient face layer. 3. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein the hot face layer comprises a base material combined with a dopant; wherein the base material comprises a material selected from the group consisting of silica, alumina, and an alumina-silica based ceramic material and wherein the dopant comprises an IR opacifier selected from the group consisting of ZrO2, SiC, rutile, T1O2, MnO, iron oxides, CrO2, ZrSiO2, AI2O3 and mixtures thereof. 4. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein a device-side low conduction volume is interposed between the back of the hot face layer and the front of the intermediate layer. 5. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 4, wherein infrared reflective foil is disposed on the back surface of the hot face layer. 6. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein an ambient-side low conduction volume is interposed between the back of the intermediate layer and the front of the ambient face layer. 7. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein a device-side low conduction volume is interposed between the back of the hot face layer and the front of the intermediate layer, and wherein an ambient-side low conduction volume is interposed between the back of the intermediate layer and the front of the ambient face layer. 8. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 4, wherein solids are excluded from the device-side low conduction volume. 9. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 6, wherein solids are excluded from the ambient-side low conduction volume. 10. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein the intermediate layer comprises a substance selected from the group consisting of fumed silica, alumina, aluminosilicate, oxide compounds, carbide compounds, hollow ceramic microspheres, microporous material, fibrous material and aerogel. 1 1 . (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein the intermediate layer comprises an IR opacifier selected from the group consisting of ZrO2, SiC, rutile, T1O2, MnO, iron oxides, CrO2, ZrSiO2, AI2O3 and mixtures thereof. 12. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein the intermediate layer comprises an IR opacifier selected from the group consisting of ZrO2 and SiC. 13. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein the intermediate layer comprises an intermediate layer device-side composition disposed on the side of the intermediate layer proximal to the hot face layer, and an intermediate layer ambient-side composition, disposed on the side of intermediate layer distal to the hot face layer, and the composition of the intermediate layer device-side composition differs from the intermediate layer ambient-side composition. 14. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 13, wherein the intermediate layer device-side composition comprises an IR opacifier selected from the group consisting of ZrO2, SiC, rutile, T1O2, MnO, iron oxides, CrO2, ZrSiO2, AI2O3 and combinations thereof. 15. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 14, wherein the IR opacifier content of the intermediate layer device-side composition is greater than the IR opacifier content of the intermediate layer ambient-side composition. 16. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 13, further comprising a glass fiber cloth layer between the intermediate layer device-side composition and the intermediate layer ambient side composition. 17. (Original) The thermal insulation panel of claim 1 , wherein the ambient face layer comprises fused silica. 18. (Original) An insulating layer comprising a major surface and a base material combined with a dopant; wherein the base material comprises a material selected from the group consisting of silica, alumina, and an alumina-silica based ceramic material; 

 wherein the dopant comprises an IR opacifier selected from the group consisting of ZrO2, SiC, rutile, Î¤ÎÎ2, MnO, iron oxides, CrO2, ZrSiO2, AI2O3 and mixtures thereof; and wherein the insulating layer contains porosity oriented in a direction other than the minor dimension of the layer. 19. (Original) The insulating layer of claim 18, wherein the base material comprises a fused silica based material. 20. (Original) The insulating layer of claim 18 wherein the dopant comprises an IR opacifier selected from the group consisting of ZrO2 and SiC.
